name: Anthropic Messages API (caching, compaction, context editing)
context_primitives: Prompt caching reuses computation for stable prompt prefixes (system, tools, large documents) via cache_control breakpoints. Compaction (beta) summarizes older conversation blocks when inputs approach limits and drops prior messages after inserting a compaction block; enabled with beta header compact-2026-01-12 and context_management edits on supported models (e.g. Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6/4.7, Mythos preview). Context editing (separate beta context-management-2025-06-27) offers finer strategies such as clearing tool results or thinking blocks to reclaim tokens without full conversation summarization.
deployment_pattern: HTTP Messages API: attach cache_control to long static blocks; enable compaction/context_management in request JSON per docs; pair with client-side trimming of attachments for cost control.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: N/A as a product; upstream models are scored on academic long-context benchmarks separately. API features improve latency/cost and effective usable horizon for the same model.
ecosystem_integrations: Used by Claude apps, Claude Code, third-party hosts (Bedrock, Vertex), and any MCP host that calls Anthropic. Complements client-side RAG and external tool result storage.
differentiators: Server-managed compaction plus explicit caching breakpoints; distinct from 'only send smaller prompts' in that the platform can summarize and truncate safely with structured blocks.
significance_for_builders: Treat Anthropic as supplying three levers: (1) cache stable instructions and tool schemas, (2) use context editing for noisy tool I/O, (3) enable compaction for multi-hour agent transcripts instead of bespoke summarization chains.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Prompt caching documented for Messages API; compaction doc references compact-2026-01-12; context editing strategies documented for 2025-06-27 beta.
